name: Clear Float Glass
description: Standard transparent glass with high optical clarity. The most commonly used glass in construction and interior applications. Ideal for windows, shelving, and basic glazing needs.
safetyNotes: Not suitable for safety-critical applications. Breaks into sharp shards. Use toughened or laminated for safety areas.

name: Toughened Glass
description: Heat-treated safety glass that is 4-5x stronger than regular glass. When broken, it shatters into small blunt granules instead of sharp shards, making it mandatory for safety-critical applications like showers and railings.
safetyNotes: Mandatory for shower enclosures and glass doors per IS 2553 safety standards. Must be cut to size before tempering.

name: Laminated Glass
description: Two or more glass layers bonded with a PVB interlayer. Even when broken, the glass stays held together by the interlayer, providing superior safety. Essential for railings, overhead glazing, and security applications.
safetyNotes: Mandatory for overhead glazing and railings above ground floor. PVB interlayer prevents fall-through. Compliant with IS 2553 Part 3.

name: DGU / IGU (Insulated Glass)
description: Double Glazed Unit consisting of two glass panes separated by an air/argon gas-filled spacer. Provides excellent thermal and acoustic insulation, making it the standard for modern commercial facades and energy-efficient buildings.
safetyNotes: Must be manufactured in controlled factory conditions. Seal integrity critical — failed seals cause fogging. Use certified DGU manufacturers only.

name: Frosted Glass
description: Glass with a translucent, satin-like finish created through acid etching. Allows light transmission while providing privacy. Available in various opacity levels for different privacy needs.
safetyNotes: Standard frosted glass is not safety glass. For partitions and doors, use frosted toughened glass for safety compliance.

name: Reflective Glass
description: Glass with a metallic oxide coating that reflects solar heat and provides a mirror-like appearance from outside. Reduces air conditioning costs and provides daytime privacy while maintaining outward visibility from inside.
safetyNotes: Reflective coating is on one surface — ensure correct orientation during installation. High reflectivity can cause heat islands — check local building codes.

name: Low-E Glass
description: Low-emissivity glass with a microscopically thin metallic coating that reflects heat while allowing visible light. The gold standard for energy-efficient buildings, significantly reducing heating and cooling costs.
safetyNotes: Soft-coat Low-E must be used in DGU configuration — coating faces the sealed cavity. Handle with care during transport — coating is delicate.

name: Back-Painted Glass
description: Toughened glass with a baked-on lacquer coating on the back surface. Available in unlimited RAL color options. Creates a sleek, modern, easy-to-clean surface perfect for kitchen backsplashes and decorative wall cladding.
safetyNotes: Must use toughened glass as base for back-painted applications in kitchens (heat proximity). Ensure proper silicone adhesive for wall mounting.

name: Acoustic Laminated Glass
description: Laminated glass with a special acoustic PVB interlayer designed to dampen sound transmission. Provides up to 42dB noise reduction, ideal for offices near highways, hospitals, and anywhere noise control is critical.
safetyNotes: For maximum acoustic performance, use in DGU configuration with different glass thicknesses to avoid resonance frequencies.

name: Switchable Smart Glass
description: Electrically switchable glass that changes from opaque to transparent at the flick of a switch. Uses PDLC (Polymer Dispersed Liquid Crystal) film technology. The ultimate in privacy control for premium applications.
safetyNotes: Requires electrical connection (48V AC typically). Must be installed by certified smart glass installers. Include backup power for privacy-critical applications.

name: Ceramic Printed Glass
description: Toughened glass with digitally printed ceramic frit designs baked into the surface. Allows unlimited design possibilities — patterns, images, gradients. The ceramic ink is permanent and scratch-resistant.
safetyNotes: Ceramic frit printing must be done before tempering process. Design artwork must be provided in vector format for best results.

name: Bent / Curved Glass
description: Glass heated to softening point and bent to precise curved shapes. Can be tempered after bending for safety. Used in architectural applications requiring curved glass surfaces — facades, revolving doors, and display showcases.
safetyNotes: Minimum bend radius varies by thickness — consult manufacturer. Tolerance of ±2mm on curved dimensions. Longer lead times than flat glass.
